game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **WORMS** Worms is a turn-based tactical game where players control a team of 4 worms on a destructible 2D battlefield. During a player's turn, they have 30 seconds to move using on-screen left/right arrows, jump by swiping up, and select a weapon from a UI menu. Weapons like the Bazooka (dealing 25-50 HP damage) or Holy Hand Grenade (75 HP damage) are aimed by dragging a finger across the screen to adjust trajectory and power, then released to fire, with wind direction displayed on the UI affecting projectiles. The objective is to eliminate all opposing worms, each starting with 100 HP, by reducing their health to zero or knocking them into environmental hazards like water. Completing 50 single-player challenges unlocks new weapons, customization items, and environments, while a "Body Count" mode offers an endless survival challenge against AI with increasing difficulty.
game_info.description: Command your team of 4 worms in tactical turn-based warfare on a destructible battlefield. Each worm has 100 HP. Aim and fire weapons like Bazookas and Grenades to damage enemy worms or knock them into water hazards. Eliminate all enemy worms to win! The battlefield terrain is destructible - explosions create craters. Watch the wind indicator as it affects projectile trajectories.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Left/A: Move worm left
Arrow Right/D: Move worm right
Arrow Up/W: Jump
Space: Cycle through weapons (Bazooka, Grenade, Airstrike)
Z: Aim weapon left (decrease angle)
S: Aim weapon right (increase angle)
Shift: Increase power
Arrow Down: Decrease power
Enter: Fire weapon / Start game
ESC: Pause/Unpause
R: Restart (from game over screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Basic gameplay mechanics test. Verifies that worms can move, jump, and switch between weapons. Tests that the turn system works correctly and time limits are enforced. Ensures UI elements display properly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move the active worm left and right. Perform jumps. Cycle through all available weapons using Space. Wait for turn timer to expire and verify turn switches to the next worm. Repeat for a few turns.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Worm should move smoothly with physics. Jumps should work when on ground. Weapon cycling should update the UI. Turn timer should count down and automatically switch turns when reaching zero. No errors should occur during basic interactions.

TEST_2.test_description: Win condition test. Verifies that eliminating all enemy worms triggers the victory state. Tests weapon firing mechanics, damage application, and health depletion leading to worm elimination.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Fire weapons at enemy worms repeatedly with maximum power and optimal angles. Focus on one enemy worm at a time until its health reaches zero. Continue until all four enemy worms are eliminated. Monitor the game state transition.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Weapons should fire with visible projectiles and explosions. Explosions should deal damage to nearby worms, reducing their health. When a worm's health reaches zero, it should be removed from play. When all enemy worms are eliminated, the game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state.

TEST_3.test_description: Terrain destruction test. Verifies that explosions create craters in the destructible terrain, removing terrain bodies from the physics world. Tests that worms can fall through destroyed terrain.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Explosions should visibly remove terrain segments, creating craters. The physics world should update to reflect terrain changes. Worms should fall through destroyed terrain gaps. Terrain destruction should persist across turns.
TEST_3.description: Terrain destruction test. Verifies that explosions create craters in the destructible terrain, removing terrain bodies from the physics world. Tests that worms can fall through destroyed terrain.
TEST_3.expected: Explosions should visibly remove terrain segments, creating craters. The physics world should update to reflect terrain changes. Worms should fall through destroyed terrain gaps. Terrain destruction should persist across turns.

TEST_4.test_description: Wind effect test. Verifies that wind affects projectile trajectories. Tests that wind changes between turns and is displayed correctly in the UI. Ensures different wind strengths produce noticeable trajectory changes.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Wind should change value between turns. The UI should display current wind direction and strength. Projectiles should curve noticeably in the direction of the wind. Stronger wind values should produce more pronounced curves in trajectory.
TEST_4.description: Wind effect test. Verifies that wind affects projectile trajectories. Tests that wind changes between turns and is displayed correctly in the UI. Ensures different wind strengths produce noticeable trajectory changes.
TEST_4.expected: Wind should change value between turns. The UI should display current wind direction and strength. Projectiles should curve noticeably in the direction of the wind. Stronger wind values should produce more pronounced curves in trajectory.

TEST_5.test_description: Water hazard test. Verifies that worms falling into water at the bottom of the screen are eliminated from play. Tests that water hazard detection works correctly and removes worms from both teams.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: When a worm's position falls below the water level threshold, it should be immediately removed from play. The worm count for that team should decrease. Water eliminations should count toward win/loss conditions just like health depletion.
TEST_5.description: Water hazard test. Verifies that worms falling into water at the bottom of the screen are eliminated from play. Tests that water hazard detection works correctly and removes worms from both teams.
TEST_5.expected: When a worm's position falls below the water level threshold, it should be immediately removed from play. The worm count for that team should decrease. Water eliminations should count toward win/loss conditions just like health depletion.

TEST_6.test_description: Collision and damage test. Verifies that explosions damage multiple worms within blast radius. Tests that damage scales with distance from explosion center. Ensures friendly fire is enabled.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: All worms within the explosion radius should take damage. Worms closer to the explosion center should take more damage than those farther away. Friendly fire should be active, damaging the player's own worms. Health bars should accurately reflect damage taken.
TEST_6.description: Collision and damage test. Verifies that explosions damage multiple worms within blast radius. Tests that damage scales with distance from explosion center. Ensures friendly fire is enabled.
TEST_6.expected: All worms within the explosion radius should take damage. Worms closer to the explosion center should take more damage than those farther away. Friendly fire should be active, damaging the player's own worms. Health bars should accurately reflect damage taken.

TEST_7.test_description: Pause and restart functionality test. Verifies that ESC key properly pauses and unpauses the game. Tests that R key restarts from game over states. Ensures game state transitions are handled correctly.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: ESC should toggle between PLAYING and PAUSED states. Physics and game logic should freeze during pause. Game should resume correctly after unpause. R key should reset the game from game over states, returning to the start screen where ENTER can begin a new game.
